projectName: ProfeLedesma — Taller de Preprocesamiento de Datos para ML
projectDescription: An educational Python workshop covering time-series data preprocessing techniques for Machine Learning, using real GHI (Global Horizontal Irradiance) solar irradiance measurements as a hands-on case study.
keyFeatures: Step-by-step data preprocessing pipeline for ML time-series data
GHI (Global Horizontal Irradiance) solar irradiance data cleaning
Outlier detection and exploratory data analysis
Solar geometry calculations (zenith angle, TOA irradiance, air mass)
Statistical error metrics: MBE, MAE, RMSD, KSI, OVER, SS4
Model bias correction via linear regression
publicApiSurface: Geo(range_dates, lat, long, gmt, alt, beta) — solar geometry DataFrame
Geo.getCTZ(delta, omega) — cosine of solar zenith angle
Geo.to_csv(name) — export geometry DataFrame to CSV
QC(df) — applies physical quality-control filters to a GHI DataFrame
rmsd(true, pred) — Root Mean Square Deviation
KSI_OVER(Xval, Xest) — Kolmogorov-Smirnov Integral and OVER metric
Site(cod) — site metadata lookup by station code
